description_for_human: Normalize and reconcile financial data across different providers using semantic field mapping and canonical schemas.
description_for_model: Use this plugin to map fields between different financial data providers, normalize values to a common format, reconcile data from multiple sources, and retrieve canonical schemas for standardized financial data representation. Essential for ensuring data consistency across FactSet, S&P Global, Bloomberg, and other providers.

functions.name: map_fields
functions.description: Map field names from one data provider schema to another or to a canonical schema.

source_provider.description: Source data provider (e.g., factset, sp_global, bloomberg, refinitiv)

target_provider.description: Target data provider or canonical for the standard schema

fields.description: List of field names to map (e.g., totalRevenue, net_income, EBITDA)

data_category.description: Category of data: financials, market_data, estimates, or all

functions.name: normalize_values
functions.description: Normalize financial values to a standard format including currency, units, and fiscal period alignment.

values.description: Key-value pairs of field names and raw values to normalize

source_provider.description: Source data provider for context on value formats

target_currency.description: Target currency for normalization (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP)

target_units.description: Target units: ones, thousands, millions, or billions

fiscal_period_end.description: Fiscal period end date for calendar alignment (e.g., 2024-12-31)

functions.name: reconcile_sources
functions.description: Reconcile and compare data from multiple providers, identifying discrepancies and producing a best estimate.

ticker.description: Stock ticker symbol for the company being analyzed

data_points.description: Array of data points from different providers with source, field, and value

reconciliation_method.description: Method for reconciliation: average, median, primary_source, or weighted

primary_source.description: Preferred primary source when using primary_source method

tolerance.description: Acceptable variance threshold as a percentage (e.g., 0.05 for 5%)

functions.name: get_canonical_schema
functions.description: Retrieve the canonical schema definition for a specific financial data category.

data_category.description: Category of data: income_statement, balance_sheet, cash_flow, market_data, estimates, ratios, or all

include_mappings.description: Whether to include provider-specific field mappings (default: true)

providers.description: List of providers to include mappings for (e.g., factset, sp_global, bloomberg)
